name: Minnie Riperton
abstract: Minnie Julia Riperton (November 8, 1947 – July 12, 1979) was an American soul singer and songwriter best known for her 1974 single "Lovin' You", her five-octave vocal range, and her use of the whistle register. Born in 1947, Riperton grew up in Chicago's Bronzeville neighborhood on the South Side.

concerts.label: Herbie Hancock, with Special Guest Minnie Riperton

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded with her. It was almost like a death knell, after I played with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
